title: Locked Out? Try These 5 Things
excerpt: Locked out of your home? Try these tips and contact Be Secure Locksmith for convenient mobile locksmith services.
author: Netta Kaiden
metaTitle: What To Do When You're Locked Out Of Your House
metaDescription: Locked out of your home? Try these tips and contact Be Secure Locksmith for convenient mobile locksmith services.
body: At one point or another, almost everyone has experienced the dread and inconvenience of realizing their keys are somewhere else, and they’re locked out of their home. When you want to retreat into the comfort of your home without delay, being locked out can be more than a nuisance. Before panicking, however, try these tips from Be Secure Locksmith Gainesville. Still can’t get in? Call us, and we’ll be there in a hurry with our mobile locksmith service.

Maybe you or another person hid a key in a safe place around the property. Or, perhaps, left a key with someone. If anyone else could have a copy of your key, call them. It may be a family member, friend, significant other, landlord, or neighbor. Providing someone you trust with a copy of your key can prove convenient in the case of an emergency lockout. If no one has a duplicate key, you can try gaining access to the home on your own.

Before taking any drastic measures, go around the full exterior of your home and check every window and door, including sliding glass doors, to see whether they might be open. If you don’t have an extra lock on a sliding glass door, these can often be weak points in your home’s entry. If you’re able to jimmy any door open, consider installing better locks. Of course, if someone sees you trying to break into your own home, you may have to end up explaining yourself.

If you have access to the proper tools and are comfortable working on your own lock, you could try to remove the doorknob. If you’re following a tutorial, proceed with extra care because damaging the locking mechanism can require lock repair or replacement, depending on the extent of the damage. Removing the doorknob also takes a bit of time, and you run the risk of being misinterpreted as a burglar by a concerned neighbor.

Calling a mobile locksmith is often the best way to gain entry into your home without causing any damage. Our locksmiths are even available for home emergency lockouts. If you’re unable to locate your keys, make sure to have your lock rekeyed or replaced to keep your home safe from intruders.

If lockouts are a regular occurrence for you, consider switching to more secure electronic locks, which use codes instead of keys. Depending on the model, you can even set different codes for different people and delete codes as necessary. Electronic locks are more reasonably priced today than ever.
